success_surface: Row filtering and formatted copy operation with proper error handling and formatting preservation
effective_behaviors: Comprehensive pre-execution inspection of spreadsheet structure before writing logic
Type-safe date parsing with fallback string-to-datetime conversion
Preservation of all cell formatting attributes (font, fill, border, number_format, alignment) during cell copying
Proper row height management via worksheet-level dimensions rather than cell-level attributes
Column width inheritance from source range to output range before data population
Defensive coding against None/null values and empty cells in source data
Incremental tracking of written rows with statistical logging for validation
Conditional header copying only on first matching data row to avoid duplication
evidence: Agent ran multiple inspection queries to understand header location (row 5 vs row 3), data starting row (row 6), and criteria cell types before implementing filter logic
Initial attempt to copy row_height via src_cell.row_height caused AttributeError, corrected by using ws.row_dimensions[source_row].height
All five cell style properties explicitly copied: font, fill, border, number_format, alignment
Date type check with try-except block strptime fallback for non-standard datetime formats
Final output included explicit row count and output range reporting for verification

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Structure Discovery
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect worksheet structure programmatically before coding transformation logic
memory_items.generalization: Query sheet names, identify header/data row boundaries, verify criterion cell types, and confirm total dimensions prior to implementing manipulation code. This prevents assumptions about layout that cause runtime failures.

memory_items.title: Formatting Preservation During Cell Copy
memory_items.lesson: Copy all visible cell formatting attributes when duplicating content across ranges
memory_items.generalization: When transferring cell data between locations, preserve font, fill pattern, borders, number format, and text alignment using library-native copy operations. Row/column dimensions require workspace-level dimension objects, not cell attributes.

memory_items.title: Safe Type Conversion for Comparison Operations
memory_items.lesson: Validate and normalize data types before performing comparisons or calculations
memory_items.generalization: Implement defensive type-checking with exception handling when converting external data (strings, mixed formats) to expected types like datetime or numeric. Include null-value guards to skip incomplete records gracefully.

memory_items.title: Dimension Inheritance Before Data Population
memory_items.lesson: Set column widths and row heights before writing bulk data to maintain visual consistency
memory_items.generalization: When creating a mirrored output section, copy source column/row dimensions first, then populate data. This prevents manual formatting adjustments post-execution and ensures the output matches source appearance.

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Pattern Applications
- **Pre-Execution Discovery**: Always programmatically inspect sheet structure (header row, data start row, criterion cell types) before writing transformation logic to avoid incorrect assumptions about layout.
- **Dimension Access Correctness**: Row and column dimensions are accessed via `ws.row_dimensions[row]` and `ws.column_dimensions[col]`, not through cell attributes like `cell.row_height`.
- **Full Formatting Replication**: When copying cell content across ranges, replicate all style properties: font, fill, border, number_format, and alignment using the library's native copy mechanisms.
- **Defensive Type Handling**: Implement try-except blocks around type conversions (especially strings to datetime) and include null checks before processing values to skip malformed records without crashing.
- **Output Tracking**: Log row counts and output ranges after completion for independent verification of transformation accuracy.
